output.summary: Two CSS-level changes: (1) Limit footer address items to two per row on desktop by adding flex-wrap and 50% basis to the address container/items, (2) Change social media icon hover color from blue to accent color.

fileChanges.description: Add flex-wrap and width constraint to address items container so only two addresses fit per row on desktop
fileChanges.details: On the address items container (line 105), change `flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-8` to `flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:flex-wrap gap-8`. On each address item div (line 107), add `sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)]` (half width minus half of gap-8=2rem) to force two-per-row. The calc accounts for the gap-8 (2rem) between items: each item gets 50% minus half the gap so two items plus one gap fill the row exactly.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the TSX address layout changes in PHP frontend template
fileChanges.details: On line 50, change `flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-8` to `flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:flex-wrap gap-8`. On line 53, change `text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans` to `text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)]` to constrain each address to half-width.

fileChanges.description: Change social link hover color from blue to accent
fileChanges.details: On line 223, change `text-white hover:text-blue` to `text-white hover:text-accent`. This uses the theme's accent color CSS custom property which adapts per color scheme.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the hover color change in PHP frontend template
fileChanges.details: On line 32, change `text-white hover:text-blue` to `text-white hover:text-accent` in the anchor class attribute.

designDecisions.decision: Use flex-wrap with 50% basis (minus gap) to limit addresses to two per row on desktop
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'On desktop, fit only two addresses per row in the footer info section'

designDecisions.decision: Change social icon hover from hover:text-blue to hover:text-accent so it uses the theme accent color variable
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Social media link hover color should be the accent color'. The accent color is defined as --accent-color in colors.css (default neon-green, adapts per color scheme). The text-accent utility is already defined.
